titleStmt.title: Saudi Arabia 2022 – Speaker 14 – Education (Part 2)
WIBARAB Corpus
titleStmt.funder: Funded by the European Research Council under the Grant Agreement 101020127

publicationStmt.distributor: Austrian Center for Digital Humanities and Cultural Heritage

recording.p: Recorded during Saudi Arabia Fieldwork Campaign Gunda Kinzl 2022

prefixDef.p: Private URIs using the teiCorpusHeader prefix are pointers to any element in the WIBARAB teiCorpus document.
Private URIs using the share prefix are pointers to audio file residing on the WIBARAB network share.
Private URIs using the geo prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a place element in VICAV Geodata list.
Private URIs using the dmp prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB DMP document..
Private URIs using the zotid prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on a biblStruct element in the TEI export of the VICAV Zotero Group library.
Private URIs using the sources prefix are pointers to the xml:id attribute on an element in the WIBARAB list of sources.

spanGrp.span: A nurse like so, she would enter in the sector with men, and...
Because those who wanted to pursue higher education, the Bachelor studies, this was in the city
In the city she would need a place
she would need someone to stay with her
this means that the girl would not go alone
there weren't any dormitories, there weren't any governmental dormitories
they would harm ??
if she went, they would rent with her and someone would be with her, most of them would stay in flats
They would stay with the girls, an older woman would stay with them
Because we had my father's flat there, there in the city
Correct, it was old and such, but it got renovated
and we stayed there, and a group of girls came and stayed with us as well
They stayed, every now and then one of them would come and stay with us, it was like that
But this thing, there weren't many, not everybody could prepare (for this kind of set up)
meaning a few people
meaning he who could accept this
would go, but most of them here stayed here
they didn't finish their studies
They stayed at home, as was the norm, meaning they didn't finish their studies
Then, what did they do? Did they get married, or what?
Yes, that's it, she would get married and live...
and that's it
She would live her life, yes
The rest, most of them, there were people who worked in schools, this school was the only one
the only one, it served the entire area
in the days when we used to study
it was the only one, it served as both as an elementary and as a secondary school
but the highschool was in Khaybar
as well, they will travel
They would educate, they would educate their daughters in Khaybar
Afterwards, the daughters of the Rashayda started coming here
There were Egyptians, teachers, teachers from Khaybar would come to us
The girls in Khaybar were educated before the Rashayda girls,
yes
And what was the reason?
The reason was that when the institute was opened
in their region, they instantly started to become educated
Do you see? And additionally, there was the perspective that our girls should not go there, and such.
But then this was over, they went there
When the girls of the Rashayda graduated they started to teach here
Additionally, there weren't, there weren't many teachers
They did not see the need for them
And as for specializations, there was specializiation, there was specialization, there were possibly five, if I remember
Their ages are now possibly
their age is now as such, that they have retired
yes
Good, now there is...
But all these affairs occurred not so long ago
No, not so long ago, but I will tell you, in the beginning, they would study and become workers
Nursing, in the beginning in my time
there was absolutely no nursing (positions for women)
yes, for how could a woman be nurse and be around men and I don't know what else? No, they did not want this
Additionally, they got the position, my sisters got the teaching position as well, and she who got a position in the south
there wasn't the need, she would leave the position in the south and afterwards she would look for a transfer
she comes, we say she comes, meaning
she gets a transfer to here
she would come back to her region (family)
she would come, meaning she would return
you see
God's blessings on Muhammad
afterwards, the schools started to open en mass
there was a school here, but only one, as I told you already, afterwards it opened here, in Farādsa.
as I told you, here, the areas of Ḏība and Farādsa
An elementary school was opened here...
So there was a school for Farādsa and a school for aḏ-Ḏība
yes, yes and it was like this
it became normal that girls would study at the institute, they study for three years
The departments (their courses) were four years old
The departments would start to teach higher levels, the middle school and highschool
That's it, what did they start to do? The Rashidi girls started to attend schools
Additionally, there were female teachers who would come to us from other regions
To the big school and with altering training
(talking on the phone) yes, yes, yes finalize it
